mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.33% is elevated, directly increasing the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raising corporate borrowing costs. This persistently high rate compresses valuations and depresses S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains elevated, irrespective of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns fails to be negative (i.e., IC >= 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of negative returns when FEDFUNDS > 5% is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of at least 0.5 percentage points over six months signals labor market deterioration, leading to reduced consumer income and spending, weaker corporate earnings, and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by >= 0.5 percentage points over the latest 6 months
regime_hint: any regime, stronger in late cycle
failure_condition: Mean 60-day return following the trigger is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50% over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts and retail sales rise month-over-month simultaneously, it confirms synchronized strength across interest-rate-sensitive and consumption-driven demand components, amplifying positive signals for corporate profits and driving S&P 500 gains over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST_change * RSAFS_change) is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) in a linear regression of 20-day returns, or the joint signal does not produce positive returns in at least 60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, three consecutive months of rising industrial production indicate capacity constraints and increasing input costs that squeeze profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This mechanism reverses in early-cycle periods where rising production is positive.
failure_condition: During periods identified as late-cycle (output gap > 1% and UNRATE < 4%), the mean 20-day return following the trigger is non-negative (p > 0.05) or the regime-conditional model does not outperform an unconditional model by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI growth exceeds 0.5% (roughly 6% annualized), it signals persistent inflation that will prompt the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy further, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following high CPI prints are not negative in at least 60% of occurrences, or the signal does not add incremental predictive power beyond using FEDFUNDS alone (incremental AUC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales reflects a self-reinforcing cycle of job creation and consumer spending, providing a powerful signal of sustained economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS_change * RSAFS_change) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day returns, or the combined signal achieves a lower Sharpe ratio than either single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment below 4%, the current 3.9% rate already signals the economy is at full capacity. Further declines would exacerbate wage inflation and compress profit margins, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE < 4%, the mean 5-day forward return is non-negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (at least 10% below its 6-month moving average) signals a rapidly weakening housing market, which typically precedes broader economic contractions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as consumer wealth and construction activity decline.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by >= 10% from its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Average 60-day return following a housing starts decline trigger is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50% over the full sample.
